title: Wrap clearIfRemoved body in batch() and add backend sync test
analysis:
The clearIfRemoved function (selectionStore.ts:83-88) sequentially calls selectEntity(null) then hoverEntity(null) when both match. In SolidJS, each setState triggers the createEffect synchronously, so selectEntity(null) fires an immediate dispatch (selection-only path) with hoveredEntity still at the old value, then hoverEntity(null) fires a second debounced dispatch. This is two backend syncs for one logical operation.

Wrapping both calls in batch() causes SolidJS to defer effect execution until both state updates are applied. The effect then sees both selectionChanged and hoverChanged as true in a single run, hitting the debounce path — one dispatch after 100ms with both values null.

The existing test at line 403 ('clearIfRemoved dispatches when both fields match') explicitly asserts the current double-dispatch behavior: an immediate dispatch with {selectedEntity: null, hoveredEntity: 'X'}, then a debounced dispatch with both null. After the batch fix, this test must be updated to assert a single debounced dispatch with both null. This updated assertion serves as the failing test in TDD step 1.

batch is already used in other stores (claudeStore.ts, engineStore.ts) and in the test file itself, so this is an established pattern.

steps.description: Update the existing test 'clearIfRemoved dispatches when both fields match' (line 403) to assert the new batched behavior: after clearIfRemoved('X') with both fields set to 'X', expect zero immediate invokes (no intermediate dispatch with hoveredEntity still 'X'), then after advancing 100ms expect exactly one invoke with {selectedEntity: null, hoveredEntity: null}. This test will FAIL against the current unbatched implementation because selectEntity(null) currently triggers an immediate dispatch before hoverEntity(null) runs.

In selectionStore.ts: (1) add 'batch' to the import from 'solid-js' (line 1), and (2) wrap the body of clearIfRemoved in batch(() => { ... }) so both setState calls are applied atomically before the createEffect runs. This makes the failing test pass — only one debounced dispatch fires.

design_decisions.decision: Update existing test rather than adding a duplicate
design_decisions.rationale: The test 'clearIfRemoved dispatches when both fields match' at line 403 already tests the exact scenario (both fields set to same path, call clearIfRemoved). After the batch fix, its assertions become wrong. Updating it to assert the correct batched behavior serves as the TDD failing test. Adding a separate test for the same scenario would create a contradiction (two tests asserting opposite behavior for the same call).

design_decisions.decision: The batched clearIfRemoved hits the debounce path (100ms), not immediate dispatch
design_decisions.rationale: When batch() groups both changes, the effect sees both selectionChanged=true and hoverChanged=true. The effect's branching logic dispatches immediately only when selectionChanged && !hoverChanged. Since hoverChanged is true, it takes the else branch (debounce at 100ms). This is correct — there's no urgency for a removal clear, and it avoids an intermediate stale state being sent to the backend.

reuse.how: Already used in other stores to group multiple setState calls. Same import pattern: add 'batch' to the 'solid-js' import destructure.

reuse.what: Existing test infrastructure (fake timers, mockInvoke, createRoot pattern)
reuse.how: The backend sync describe block already has beforeEach/afterEach with fake timers, mockInvoke setup, and createRoot. The updated test uses the same clearIfRemoved binding (line 177-188) and assertion patterns.
